title: Basic Earnings Per Share
event: Reyes Co. reports net income of $84,000, declares $4,000 of preferred dividends, and has 40,000 weighted-average common shares outstanding. Compute basic earnings per share.

computationPaths.narration: EPS belongs to the COMMON shareholder — so the preferred claim comes off the top before dividing.

memorize.body: Basic EPS = (Net income − preferred dividends) ÷ weighted-average common shares outstanding.
Weighted-average shares, not year-end shares — mid-year issuances count only for the months they existed.

questions.prompt: NI $84,000, preferred dividends $4,000, weighted-average shares 40,000. Basic EPS = ?

distractors.feedback: You skipped the preferred dividends. EPS measures the COMMON shareholders' slice: (84,000 − 4,000) ÷ 40,000.
You ADDED the preferred dividends. They're a claim ahead of common — subtract: 80,000 ÷ 40,000 = 2.00.
